title: coleus trullatus
intro: Coleus trullatus, also known as Coleus aromaticus, is a tropical plant species native to Asia, belonging to the Lamiaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include being used to treat fever, colds, and digestive issues due to its anthelmintic, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties. Coleus trullatus offers various health benefits, such as relieving symptoms of respiratory infections, reducing inflammation, and aiding in the treatment of skin conditions like eczema and acne. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, with herbal preparations typically involving the leaves and stems being dried and then used in various forms.
